rxn_class_summary: Metal-free electrocatalytic iodide-mediated cascade bicyclization of N-allyl enamine carboxylates to give [3.1.0]-azabicycles and γ-butyrolactone-fused 1-pyrrolines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to a hypervalent/cationic I+ species which acts as the active electrophilic mediator; cathodic reduction of protons to H2 maintains charge balance

key_intermediates.name: I+ (cationic iodide / hypervalent iodine species) generated by anodic oxidation of I-
iodonium-activated alkene (iodiranium on pendant allyl of enamine)
iodocyclized pyrroline intermediate B (5-exo-tet cyclized C-I pyrroline)
tautomerized pyrroline intermediate C bearing CH2I and enamine ester

electron_flow: At the graphite anode, I- is oxidized (-2e-) to I+, which activates the pendant allyl alkene of the N-allyl enamine to trigger 5-exo-tet iodocyclization to a C-I pyrroline; tautomerization then enables either intramolecular SN2 cyclopropanation (regenerating I-) to give the [3.1.0]-azabicycle, or, for tetrasubstituted enamines, ester O-attack/dealkylation to give the γ-butyrolactone-fused pyrroline. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Anodic electro-oxidation of iodide to I+ (CV shows oxidation peaks at 0.86, 1.20, and 1.43 V required for catalyst turnover)

probe_reagents.name: molecular I2 (catalytic or stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: No formation of 2a with I2 alone (cat. or stoich.), confirming that electrochemical oxidation to I+ is required and that I2 itself is not the active species

probe_reagents.name: dry MeCN (no water)
probe_reagents.observation: Diminished yield of 2a in absence of water; water aids KI solubility, stabilizes charged intermediates, and facilitates proton mobility

probe_reagents.name: O2 / Ar atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Yields under air (84%), O2 (85%), and Ar (81%) are essentially identical, ruling out aerobic oxidation and supporting a non-radical (ionic) pathway

probe_reagents.name: isolated iodocyclized intermediate 5a' (cis)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated with 1 equiv KI at shorter time; under standard conditions cis-5a' lactonizes to 4a while trans-5a is recovered, confirming the iodocyclized pyrroline as a productive intermediate

probe_reagents.name: putative alcohol 6a
probe_reagents.observation: Not detected, excluded as an intermediate in lactone 4 formation

notes: Atmosphere-independent yields and isolation of iodocyclized pyrrolines argue against a radical pathway and support an ionic iodonium/5-exo-tet mechanism. Divergence between [3.1.0]-azabicycle (R2 = H, cyclopropanation) and γ-butyrolactone-fused pyrroline (R2 = alkyl, ester annulation/dealkylation) is rationalized by substrate substitution. Borneol ester gave only modest dr (1:1.27), suggesting weak stereocontrol in the cyclization step.
